hyperedges.label: WCAG AA Contrast Safety System (commitment, safe color steps, cautions, measured failures)
Favicon Applies the Heirloom Album Brand Palette

nodes.label: Home Page Design Critique (24/40)
nodes.rationale: Dual-agent critique (design-review + detector-evidence) of app/page.tsx scoring 24/40: system primitives are real and obeyed, but the page assembles them into the generic 2024-26 template scroll, undermining the brand for the design-literate audience the studio sells to.

nodes.label: P1: Mobile Hero Grid Empty Holes
nodes.rationale: Hero.tsx:61 uses grid-cols-2 below lg with 1/2/1-span children in source order, leaving empty cells; matters because leads arrive warm from Instagram overwhelmingly on phones, so the first impression on the majority device looks broken.
nodes.norm_label: p1: mobile hero grid empty holes

nodes.label: P1: Conversion Hierarchy Inverts Documented Strategy
nodes.rationale: Hero fills 'Book a session' and outlines the portfolio while PRODUCT.md's primary CTA is 'View portfolio'; four different labels (Book a Session, Book a session, See packages, Check availability) all route to /booking, breaking information scent with salesman-first ordering the brand rejects.
nodes.norm_label: p1: conversion hierarchy inverts documented strategy

nodes.label: P1: WCAG AA Contrast Failures on Brass and Muted Text
nodes.rationale: Brass #a9743f eyebrows measure 3.74:1 on cream and muted #8b857a 3.43:1 against a needed 4.5:1, on real content; fix is a text-safe brass step (#8a5a2b = 5.49:1), moving muted text to text-ink-soft, and darkening placeholders.
nodes.norm_label: p1: wcag aa contrast failures on brass and muted text

nodes.label: P1: Reduced-Motion Promise Not Kept / Opacity-0 Gating
nodes.rationale: The global CSS rule only neutralizes CSS animation; Framer Motion (every Reveal, testimonial crossfade, rAF counter) runs at full motion under prefers-reduced-motion, and initial opacity:0 server-renders below-fold content invisible for no-JS visitors; fix is MotionConfig reducedMotion='user' and a CSS-first default-visible reveal.
nodes.norm_label: p1: reduced-motion promise not kept / opacity-0 gating

nodes.label: P2: Template Scaffolding (Eyebrow Saturation, Stat Band, Icon-Card Grid)
nodes.rationale: 7 of 8 sections carry the eyebrow (5 in an unsanctioned dash-less format), Stats.tsx is the banned hero-metric template running placeholder numbers, Services.tsx is the banned identical icon-card grid; strategically the biggest opportunity — cut scaffolding, move the work up, make Services photo-led.
nodes.norm_label: p2: template scaffolding (eyebrow saturation, stat band, icon-card grid)

nodes.label: Design Context Prerequisite (read PRODUCT.md and DESIGN.md)
nodes.rationale: Any design or UI work must start from PRODUCT.md (strategy) and DESIGN.md (visual system) because the site's job is credibility, the primary CTA is 'View portfolio', and all testimonials/stats are placeholder that must never ship as real proof.
nodes.norm_label: design context prerequisite (read product.md and design.md)

nodes.label: Contrast Cautions (--color-muted and --color-brass fail 4.5:1 on cream)
nodes.rationale: --color-muted (#8b857a) and --color-brass (#a9743f) both fail 4.5:1 on cream, so neither may be used for small body text.
nodes.norm_label: contrast cautions (--color-muted and --color-brass fail 4.5:1 on cream)

nodes.label: Graphify Query-First Workflow
nodes.rationale: Codebase questions should hit graphify query/path/explain before grep because they return a scoped subgraph much smaller than GRAPH_REPORT.md or raw source; after code changes run graphify update (AST-only, no API cost).

nodes.label: The Heirloom Album (Creative North Star)
nodes.rationale: Every screen is a spread in a hand-bound wedding album — linen-cream pages, brass foil stamping, serif captions; the system must feel kept, not scrolled past, because timelessness is the brand promise, so it optimizes for how it will look in twenty years and the site's own craft is the first proof of the studio's eye.

nodes.label: The Foil Stamp Rule
nodes.rationale: Brass appears on well under 10% of any screen, applied with foil-stamping precision (one italic word, one icon, one ring) because its rarity is its value — if brass covers areas instead of details it has stopped being foil.

nodes.label: The Photograph Owns the Color Rule
nodes.rationale: The UI palette stays paper-quiet because the photography supplies the color; no new saturated hues enter the system, and if a screen feels colorless the fix is imagery, never chrome.
nodes.norm_label: the photograph owns the color rule

nodes.label: The One Italic Rule
nodes.rationale: A display heading earns at most one italicized brass-colored word — the emotional pivot — because two makes it a costume.

nodes.label: The Eyebrow Is the Signature Rule
nodes.rationale: The brass tracked-caps eyebrow (hairline dash + label, Deep Brass) is the brand's one sanctioned kicker format with a budget of at most 3 per page — it orients, it does not scaffold every section; never invent a second kicker style.
nodes.norm_label: the eyebrow is the signature rule

nodes.label: The Flat-at-Rest Rule
nodes.rationale: Depth is interaction feedback, never decoration — surfaces at rest are separated by Deckle Line hairline borders and background tint steps, and any shadow on a static non-interactive element must be removed.

nodes.label: The Album Palette (cream/ink neutrals + brass accent)
nodes.rationale: A restrained paper-and-ink palette (Linen Cream #faf7f1, Charcoal Ink #1b1916, Brass Foil #a9743f, plus AA-safe steps Deep Brass and Deep Caption) where photography carries the color and the UI supplies warmth and one metallic voice.
nodes.norm_label: the album palette (cream/ink neutrals + brass accent)

nodes.label: Deep Brass (#8a5a2b, text-safe brass step)
nodes.rationale: Exists at 5.5:1 on cream so the foil voice survives at caption scale (eyebrows, kicker dashes, step numbers) without breaking AA, since Brass Foil #a9743f sits at only 3.7:1.

nodes.label: Deep Caption (#6b665e, readable metadata gray)
nodes.rationale: The AA-safe metadata gray (5.2:1 on cream) for trust rows, legal lines, testimonial roles and placeholders, because Faded Caption #8b857a at 3.4:1 fails AA and is reserved for decorative traces only.

nodes.label: Playfair Display + Inter Editorial Pairing
nodes.rationale: A high-contrast transitional serif that reads like an album caption plate over a self-effacing sans that handles functional text without personality competition — the serif speaks, the sans serves.

nodes.label: Reveal Motion Primitive (signature motion)
nodes.rationale: Every section enters through a fade + 26px rise (0.7s, once, optional sibling stagger) with one cinematic 18s ken-burns hero zoom, because motion is a slow exhale; a global prefers-reduced-motion rule is supposed to collapse all animation to near-instant.

nodes.label: Product Purpose: Close Warm Leads via Credibility
nodes.rationale: The site exists to close leads that originate elsewhere — referrals and social drive warm traffic — so success is credibility: the portfolio convinces, services show fit, and contact/booking make the next step easy; the /admin dashboard stays functional but explicitly secondary.
nodes.norm_label: product purpose: close warm leads via credibility

nodes.label: Primary CTA: View Portfolio
nodes.rationale: Lead visitors into the work first because the work does the selling; 'Get in touch / Book a session' is the secondary, low-pressure next step once convinced.

nodes.label: Belief Ladder (beautiful work → fits my event → reliable → easy to reach)
nodes.rationale: Four beliefs the site must build in order: the work is genuinely beautiful (portfolio), they can handle MY kind of event (breadth), they're reliable professionals (process/testimonials), and reaching out is easy and low-pressure (clear contact, transparent packages).
nodes.norm_label: belief ladder (beautiful work → fits my event → reliable → easy to reach)

nodes.label: Anti-Reference: Generic Template Look
nodes.rationale: Interchangeable agency/SaaS landing page — stock sections, icon grids, could be anyone's site — banned because a studio selling taste loses the sale at the aesthetic layer.

nodes.label: Anti-Reference: Cheesy Wedding-Vendor
nodes.rationale: Script fonts, rose gold, heart motifs, sentimental clip-art aesthetics — the opposite of the calm editorial register.

nodes.label: Anti-Reference: Dark Moody Luxury
nodes.rationale: Near-black backgrounds, gold serif, exclusive-hotel vibes — ink is for text and small fills, never whole viewports.

nodes.label: Anti-Reference: Trendy / Maximalist
nodes.rationale: Loud gradients, oversized type experiments, brutalist or acid aesthetics that date quickly — if it would date a printed album, it dates this site.

nodes.label: Design Principle 1: The Work Does the Talking
nodes.rationale: Layout, chrome, and copy defer to the photography — the site is the portfolio's frame, never its competitor.
nodes.norm_label: design principle 1: the work does the talking

nodes.label: Design Principle 2: The Site Is Itself the Proof
nodes.rationale: A studio selling taste must demonstrate taste — every detail of the site's craft is a credibility signal for a design-literate audience.
nodes.norm_label: design principle 2: the site is itself the proof

nodes.label: Design Principle 3: Calm Confidence, Never Salesmanship
nodes.rationale: No urgency tricks, no hard sells — unhurried pacing and transparent packages are the persuasion.
nodes.norm_label: design principle 3: calm confidence, never salesmanship

nodes.label: Design Principle 4: Breadth Without Dilution
nodes.rationale: Show range across weddings, corporate, concerts, and portraits while keeping one coherent voice, because no single client type dominates.

nodes.label: Design Principle 5: Never Fake the Proof (placeholder testimonials/stats)
nodes.rationale: Current testimonials, review counts ('5.0 across 180+ reviews') and stats are scaffold copy; real proof must be collected into .impeccable/assets/proof/ and swapped in before launch — invented proof must never ship.
nodes.norm_label: design principle 5: never fake the proof (placeholder testimonials/stats)

nodes.label: WCAG 2.2 AA Commitment
nodes.rationale: At least 4.5:1 text contrast (3:1 large text), full keyboard navigation, visible focus states, and prefers-reduced-motion alternatives for all animation via the global rule in app/globals.css.

nodes.label: Timeless Visuals Website
nodes.rationale: Events photography & videography studio site — Home, Portfolio (filterable gallery + lightbox), Contact, multi-step Booking, and a lightweight /admin dashboard — built on Next.js 16 App Router with a light editorial aesthetic.

nodes.label: Booking System (one booking per day, availability calendar)
nodes.rationale: The booking page fetches taken dates from GET /api/bookings, the calendar disables past and booked days, and POST /api/bookings re-checks the date server-side (409 on race) to guard double-booking; the start time is informational only — it tells the studio when to arrive and never affects availability.
nodes.norm_label: booking system (one booking per day, availability calendar)

nodes.label: JSON File Store (/data via lib/store.ts)
nodes.rationale: Bookings and messages persist as git-ignored JSON files (bookings.json, contact.json) auto-created on first write — ideal for local/self-hosted Node; on serverless the filesystem is ephemeral, so lib/store.ts must be swapped for a database, and data access is deliberately isolated in that one file to keep the swap small.

nodes.label: Admin Dashboard (/admin)
nodes.rationale: Lists all bookings and messages; open in local development, optionally gated by ADMIN_TOKEN in .env.local and accessed as /admin?token=...

nodes.label: Tech Stack (Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind v4, Framer Motion)
nodes.rationale: Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, Framer Motion for animation, date-fns for the calendar, lucide-react icons, next/image with curated Unsplash photography.
nodes.norm_label: tech stack (next.js 16, react 19, typescript, tailwind v4, framer motion)

nodes.label: Content Single Source (lib/content.ts)
nodes.rationale: Studio info, services, packages, portfolio, testimonials, and FAQs all live in lib/content.ts so content edits need no component changes; photos are Unsplash IDs replaceable there or via /public, and brand tokens live in app/globals.css @theme.

nodes.label: Camera Lens Motif (brass lens ring, aperture dot, glint highlight on dark ink rounded square)
nodes.norm_label: camera lens motif (brass lens ring, aperture dot, glint highlight on dark ink rounded square)

nodes.label: Next.js App Router icon.svg Favicon Convention
nodes.norm_label: next.js app router icon.svg favicon convention
